title: Store Windsurf MCP config in repo-local .windsurf/ instead of global user path
context: Hunch integrates with multiple assistants (Cursor, VS Code, Codex) via committed configs. Windsurf is being added. Core question: should Windsurf config be repo-local (like Cursor) or stored in global ~/.codeium/ user path (Windsurf's default)? Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: Use repo-local .windsurf/mcp_config.json committed to git, matching Cursor's pattern. MCP server args are hardcoded in config. Both .windsurf/mcp_config.json and .windsurf/rules/hunch.md are managed by scaffoldProviders and integrated into the init flow.
consequences: All Windsurf users of this repo automatically inherit Hunch grounding via version control
Windsurf joins the set of 'first-class' assistants with repo-local Hunch integration (vs. AGENTS.md fallback)
MCP server path must be hardcoded per repo/user; no portable config
Windsurf rule uses `trigger: always_on` to persist context across all requests
alternatives_rejected: Store config in global ~/.codeium/windsurf path — allows per-user customization but breaks shared-memory model and requires per-installation setup
Use AGENTS.md only without repo-local config — loses Windsurf's native ability to consume MCP servers
